mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 45% of months where payroll growth exceeds 200k, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term negative equity returns as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in greater than 50% of months with industrial production declines, or signal reverses to positive during early-cycle recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising unemployment indicate accommodative policy failing to stimulate labor demand, creating stagflationary fears that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when rates are near zero and unemployment rises, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annually signals insufficient aggregate demand to support corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly returns exceed 55% of the time when GDP growth is below 1.5%, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy across different business cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating immediate negative short-term equity returns as discount rates adjust upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of months with CPI increases, or inflation loses its negative correlation with equity returns during supply-driven price shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during periods of stable industrial output confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive equity returns as earnings growth expectations solidify over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE MoM change < -0.2 and INDPRO MoM change > 0.0
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when unemployment falls due to labor force participation drops rather than job creation, or predictive accuracy drops below 50% in rolling windows.
